note_data.title: Heat
keyPoints: Heat is a form of energy that flows from hot body to cold body due to temperature difference
Temperature is the degree of hotness or coldness of a body measured on a definite scale
Specific heat capacity is the amount of heat required to raise the temperature of unit mass of a substance through 1°C
Heat capacity is the amount of heat required to raise the temperature of the whole body through 1°C
Latent heat of fusion is the amount of heat required to convert unit mass of solid into liquid at constant temperature
Latent heat of vaporization is the amount of heat required to convert unit mass of liquid into vapour at constant temperature
Heat is transferred by three methods: conduction, convection and radiation
Good conductors like metals have free electrons which transfer heat energy quickly
Convection occurs in liquids and gases due to density differences caused by heating
All hot bodies emit heat radiations which travel at the speed of light

definitions.definition: Heat is a form of energy that flows from a hot body to a cold body when they are in thermal contact.
Temperature is the degree of hotness or coldness of a body measured on a definite scale like Celsius or Fahrenheit.
Specific heat capacity of a substance is the amount of heat required to raise the temperature of unit mass of that substance through 1°C.
Two bodies are said to be in thermal equilibrium when there is no net flow of heat between them.
Latent heat is the amount of heat required to change the state of unit mass of a substance at constant temperature and pressure.

importantQuestions.question: Differentiate between heat and temperature.
importantQuestions.answer: Heat is a form of energy that flows due to temperature difference while temperature is degree of hotness. Heat depends on mass while temperature does not. Heat is measured in joules while temperature in °C.

importantQuestions.question: Explain the three methods of heat transfer with examples from daily life.
importantQuestions.answer: 1) Conduction: Heat transfer through solids without movement of particles. Example: heating of spoon in hot tea. 2) Convection: Heat transfer in fluids due to movement of heated particles. Example: sea breeze in Karachi. 3) Radiation: Heat transfer through electromagnetic waves. Example: heat from sun reaching earth. Each method has specific applications in daily life and industries.

Heat transfer in solids mainly occurs by:
The latent heat of fusion of ice is:

commonMistakes: Students confuse heat with temperature - remember heat is energy, temperature is degree
Forgetting to write units in numerical problems (J, cal, J/kg°C)
Mixing up latent heat of fusion (80 cal/g) with vaporization (540 cal/g)
Not mentioning 'at constant temperature' when defining latent heat
Drawing conduction diagram without showing particle vibrations
examTips: This chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs and 1 short question in every BSEK paper
Numerical problems on Q = mcΔT appear frequently - memorize the formula
Heat transfer methods diagram appears every 2-3 years
Always define terms exactly as given in STBB textbook
Practice numerical problems on specific heat and latent heat calculations
formulas: Q = mcΔT (Heat = mass × specific heat × temperature change)
C = mc (Heat capacity = mass × specific heat capacity)
note_data.keyDiagram: Heat transfer methods diagram showing: 1) Conduction in metal rod with particles vibrating 2) Convection in water with circulation arrows 3) Radiation from sun to earth with wave lines. Label each method clearly with examples.
pastPaperRefs: BSEK 2023 SSC-II Q3 (4 marks) — asked students to define heat and temperature with two differences between them
BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q7 (6 marks) — required explanation of three methods of heat transfer with one example each
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q4 (3 marks) — asked to define specific heat capacity and write its formula
BSEK 2020 SSC-II Q8 (5 marks) — calculation problem on heat energy using Q = mcΔT formula

workedExample.question: Define heat and temperature. State any two differences between them. (5 marks)
steps: Define heat using exact textbook definition
Define temperature using exact textbook definition
workedExample.answer: Heat: Heat is a form of energy that flows from a body at higher temperature to a body at lower temperature. Temperature: Temperature is the degree of hotness or coldness of a body. Differences: (1) Heat is a form of energy while temperature is the degree of hotness. (2) Heat is measured in Joules while temperature is measured in Celsius or Kelvin.
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for heat definition, 1 mark for temperature definition, 1.5 marks for each difference stated correctly

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by writing general answers instead of textbook-exact definitions. Always use the STBB textbook phrasing for heat and temperature definitions.
Many students confuse heat and temperature concepts. Remember: heat is energy transfer, temperature is degree of hotness - never interchange these terms.
In calculation problems, students forget to write the formula first before substitution. Always write Q = mcΔT, then substitute values to get full marks.
note_data.quickRecap: Heat is energy transfer from hot to cold bodies, measured in Joules. Temperature is degree of hotness, measured in Celsius/Kelvin. Heat transfers through conduction (direct contact), convection (fluid movement), and radiation (electromagnetic waves). Use Q = mcΔT for heat calculations where m is mass, c is specific heat capacity, and ΔT is temperature change.
